question_text: Why does an object float in water?

options.label: A
options.text: Because it is lighter in weight than water

options.label: B
options.text: Because its density is less than the density of water

options.label: C
options.text: Because it has a small volume

options.label: D
options.text: Because water pushes it up with a force

explanation: Objects float when their density is lower than the fluid they're in. Water has a density of 1 g/cm³, so anything less dense will float on top.
